title: goniopteris clarkei
intro: Goniopteris clarkei, also known as the Clarke's fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The Clarke's fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its extracts. In herbal preparations, the Clarke's fern is often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these benefits.
